summary: Carole Herder blog post encouraging horse owners to transition to barefoot, stressing preparation, education and a qualified trimmer before pulling shoes rather than rushing.
Brand news post announcing that Cavallo distributor Battles won the BETA International 'Trade Supplier of the Year' award for 2015, its second consecutive win.
Carole Herder reflects on essential horse equipment, arguing that Cavallo hoof boots are her must-have item for protecting horses on rough terrain.
Post covering The Caravan, a 2,500-mile transcontinental horse-powered journey across the USA in 2014-15, featuring barefoot horses and promoting therapeutic and long-distance driving.
Educational post on hay for horses, covering how cutting stage, time of day, plant species and curing affect nutritional value and fructan content, plus free-choice feeding.
Carole Herder discusses hoof landing mechanics, revisiting her advocacy of heel-first landing and reconsidering toe-first and flat landings given conflicting evidence; includes a YouTube video.
Post sharing Monty Roberts' views on barefoot horses, recounting his 2003 Rose Parade demonstration with six wild mustangs trained and shown without shoes.
Post on the Dakota 38+2 Memorial Ride, a 16-day, 330-mile barefoot journey commemorating the 1862 mass execution of 38 Dakota people, and Native American horsemanship.
Promotional post inviting readers to enter ACTHA's draw to win a trip to Australia's Equine Lifestyle Festival, with extra entries for riding in Cavallo hoof boots at ACTHA events.
Carole Herder examines modern equine deworming, advocating fecal egg counts over routine frequent worming to avoid parasite resistance and target treatment.
Post about TV show 'Best of America by Horseback' and host Tom Seay laying a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, inviting readers to add names to a tribute book honoring veterans.
Carole Herder explains how a balanced diet and minerals (zinc, copper, biotin, selenium) support healthy hooves, framing diet, environment and trim as the three pillars of hoof care.
